content: Hi, I'm Frankie Reyes, a PhD Student in Computational Linguistics. I'm feeling a bit stuck in my program and I'm thinking about making a career change. I've always been interested in marathon running and I've been training for a few months now.

It's great that you have a passion outside of your academic pursuits. Marathon running can be a great way to challenge yourself and clear your mind. What is it about your PhD program that's making you feel stuck? Is it the research itself or the academic environment?

I think it's a bit of both. I love the research, but the academic environment can be pretty competitive and demanding. I've been working on my dissertation for a few years now and I'm starting to feel like I'm losing my motivation. I've also recently started collecting vintage cars, which has been a great way for me to relax and express myself.

It's great that you've found ways to relax and express yourself. Collecting vintage cars can be a fun and rewarding hobby. Have you thought about how you could combine your passion for marathon running with your academic background in computational linguistics? Perhaps you could work on projects related to sports analytics or wearable technology.

That's an interesting idea. I've actually been thinking about starting my own business, maybe something related to mechanical keyboards. I've always been fascinated by the technology and design behind keyboards and I think it could be a great way to combine my technical skills with my passion for design.

Starting your own business can be a great way to pursue your passions. What kind of business were you thinking of starting? Would it be a keyboard design company, or maybe a business that creates custom keyboard layouts?

I'm not sure yet, I'm still in the planning stages. But I'm thinking about taking a trip to Tokyo in June to learn more about the keyboard industry and get some inspiration. I've heard that Tokyo has a vibrant tech scene and some of the best keyboard manufacturers in the world.

Tokyo is a great place to visit for tech enthusiasts. You could also consider reaching out to other keyboard enthusiasts or designers to learn more about the industry and get advice. Have you thought about how you'll fund your business venture?

Yeah, I've been saving up for a while now and I'm planning to use some of my own savings to fund the business. I'm also thinking about applying for a small business loan to help get things off the ground. My friend, Emily, has offered to help me with the business plan and financials.

It's great that you have a support system in place. Having a solid business plan and financials will be crucial to securing a loan. What kind of timeline are you thinking of for starting your business?

I'm hoping to have everything up and running by the end of the year. I'm planning to start small and see how things go from there. I've already started working on a business plan and I'm planning to launch my website by August 1st.

That's a great goal to have. Starting small and scaling up as you grow is a good way to test the waters and make sure your business is viable. I think you have a good chance of success, especially with your technical background and passion for design.

Thanks, I'm feeling pretty positive about it. I'm just hoping that I can make it work and create a successful business that I can be proud of. I'm planning to hire 1-2 employees within the first year and expand my product line to include custom keyboard layouts.

It's great that you have a clear vision for your business. Hiring employees and expanding your product line will be a big step, but it's a good way to grow and scale your business. Just make sure to take things one step at a time and don't be afraid to ask for help when you need it.

I will, thanks for the advice. I'm also thinking about moving to a new apartment in Amsterdam, maybe something with a bigger workspace for my business. I love the city and I think it would be a great place to start my business.

Amsterdam is a great city, and it's definitely a hub for tech and entrepreneurship. Having a bigger workspace will be helpful as you grow your business. Just make sure to research the area and find a place that fits your needs and budget.
